findings.title: libraryService.ts streamUrl still points to removed /api/stream/:id endpoint
findings.suggested_fix: Change line 57 from `streamUrl: \`/api/stream/${t.id}\`` to `streamUrl: \`/api/stream/${t.id}/playlist.m3u8\``. This is a hard contract break: any client that uses the streamUrl field from track/album responses (REST API consumers, third-party integrations) will receive a path that now 404s. The Flutter client currently builds its own URL in _uriForTrack() and ignores the streamUrl field, so Flutter itself is unaffected, but the field is part of the public API contract.

findings.title: Bearer-only auth path produces empty token embedded in segment URIs
findings.suggested_fix: In handlePlaylist(), `token` is extracted only from the query parameter (req.query['token']). If a client authenticates via Authorization: Bearer header and omits ?token=, the token variable is an empty string and every segment URI in the returned playlist will contain `token=` (empty). The authenticate middleware will still accept the segment request if a Bearer header is sent, but HLS demuxers (mpv, ExoPlayer, AVPlayer) load segment URLs as plain HTTP fetches without custom headers — they will fail auth. Fix: also try extracting the token from the Authorization header as fallback: `const authHeader = req.headers['authorization']; const bearerToken = authHeader?.startsWith('Bearer ') ? authHeader.slice(7) : ''; const token = (typeof req.query['token'] === 'string' ? req.query['token'] : null) ?? bearerToken;`. Document that clients MUST pass token via query param to use HLS.

findings.title: Segment URI relative resolution depends on exact playlist URL path — .m3u8 alias must end with /playlist.m3u8, not bare filename
findings.suggested_fix: Segment URIs are emitted as relative paths: `segment/000?bitrate=...`. RFC 3986 relative-reference resolution resolves them against the playlist URL's base (everything up to the last '/'). For playlist URL `http://host/api/stream/42/playlist` → base is `http://host/api/stream/42/` → resolved segment is `http://host/api/stream/42/segment/000` ✓. For `http://host/api/stream/42/playlist.m3u8` → base is `http://host/api/stream/42/` → resolved segment is `http://host/api/stream/42/segment/000` ✓. Both aliases resolve correctly. However, if any proxy or client strips the last path component differently (e.g., treats the full path as a directory), segments could mis-resolve. Verify with ExoPlayer and AVPlayer in integration testing; consider using absolute URIs in the playlist as the safest option.

findings.title: HLS TARGETDURATION must be a ceiling integer; floating-point segmentSeconds would violate spec
findings.suggested_fix: The HLS spec (RFC 8216 §4.3.3.1) requires EXT-X-TARGETDURATION to be a decimal-integer (not floating-point) and to be >= the longest segment duration rounded up. Currently `segmentSeconds` comes from env.HLS_SEGMENT_SECONDS which is an integer (default 6), so the emitted value is correct. However, the type of `segmentSeconds` in buildPlaylist() is `number`, and if HLS_SEGMENT_SECONDS were ever set to a non-integer (e.g. 6.5), the TARGETDURATION line would emit `6.5` which is spec-invalid and rejected by strict HLS validators. Add `Math.ceil(segmentSeconds)` in the template literal: `` `#EXT-X-TARGETDURATION:${Math.ceil(segmentSeconds)}` ``.

findings.title: EXT-X-MEDIA-SEQUENCE with startSegment > 0 may confuse clients that use it for discontinuity detection
findings.suggested_fix: Setting EXT-X-MEDIA-SEQUENCE to startSegment is correct per spec for encoding the absolute sequence number of the first segment. AVPlayer and ExoPlayer use this value for discontinuity detection in live streams. For VOD (EXT-X-PLAYLIST-TYPE:VOD) this field is less critical, but some older client implementations may use MEDIA-SEQUENCE only to detect live stream reloads and ignore it for VOD. The current implementation is spec-correct; this is a note to verify behavior with AVPlayer on iOS when seeking causes playlist reloads.

findings.title: Segment index route parameter accepts any parseInt-parseable string including negative values after initial < 0 check
findings.suggested_fix: The route parses `:index` with `parseInt(req.params['index'] as string, 10)`. parseInt('000', 10) = 0 and parseInt('020', 10) = 20 — both correct. The check `isNaN(segmentIndex) || segmentIndex < 0` correctly rejects negative values and NaN. However, parseInt('0xff', 10) = 0 (stops at 'x') and parseInt('10abc', 10) = 10 — these parse but are malformed inputs. Consider replacing with `Number(req.params['index'])` and checking `!Number.isInteger(n)` for strict validation, or using a regex pre-check `(/^\d{1,4}$/).test(req.params['index'])`. Minor issue with low exploitability risk since the upper bound check (>= segmentCount) limits access.

findings.title: Old GET /api/stream/:trackId removed with no deprecation period — existing installed clients break immediately
findings.suggested_fix: The old progressive stream endpoint is fully deleted with no compatibility shim. Any installed version of the Flutter app older than this release will receive 404 on all stream attempts, resulting in complete playback failure. The Flutter client builds URLs in _uriForTrack() (audio_service.dart line 680) using the new playlist.m3u8 format, so the current app version is correct. Mitigation options: (1) Add a redirect or tombstone route `GET /:trackId` → 410 Gone with `{ error: { code: 'ENDPOINT_REMOVED', message: 'Use /playlist.m3u8' } }` to give clearer errors to old clients; (2) Coordinate a forced-update mechanism (the existing auto-updater) before deploying. Severity depends on whether rollout is staged.

findings.title: streamUrl field in Track type (api.ts) may be out of sync with the new URL format
findings.suggested_fix: The Track type's `streamUrl` field should be documented or typed to reflect that it now returns an HLS playlist URL ending in /playlist.m3u8 (or /playlist), not a progressive MP3 stream URL. Any downstream consumer (docs, API clients, integration tests) expecting a directly playable MP3 URL will be broken. Check src/types/api.ts and update comments/types accordingly. Also check Flutter Track model (lib/models/track.dart) — if it has a streamUrl field, it should be treated as an HLS URL.

findings.title: No Cache-Control on segment responses — same segment served multiple times unnecessarily
findings.suggested_fix: The playlist response sets `Cache-Control: no-store` (correct — token is embedded). However, segment responses (served via serveFile()) do not set explicit cache headers. Since segment URLs contain the token (user-specific) and segments are immutable once generated (content-addressed by trackKey), `Cache-Control: private, max-age=3600` would allow the HLS client to cache segments in-memory without serving stale content. This is an optimization, not a correctness issue.

residual_risks: Any third-party HLS clients or REST API consumers using the `streamUrl` field from track responses will silently receive the old /api/stream/:id path (which now 404s) because libraryService.ts was not updated.
Clients that authenticate via Bearer header only (not ?token= query param) will receive playlists with empty token values in segment URIs, causing auth failures when the HLS demuxer fetches segments.
Older installed app versions will completely lose playback capability on backend upgrade with no graceful fallback or user-facing error message.

The hardcoded `_segmentDuration = Duration(seconds: 6)` in the Flutter client (audio_service.dart line 232) is a magic constant that must stay in sync with the backend HLS_SEGMENT_SECONDS env var. A mismatch causes incorrect seek position calculations.
EXT-X-TARGETDURATION compliance could break if HLS_SEGMENT_SECONDS is ever set to a non-integer value via environment configuration.
testing_gaps: No integration test verifies that a Bearer-header-authenticated client receives a playlist with a non-empty token in segment URIs.
No test validates the relative URI resolution behavior of the emitted segment paths across different HLS client implementations (AVPlayer on iOS, ExoPlayer on Android, mpv on Windows).
No test covers the case where startSegment equals segmentCount - 1 (last valid segment) or startSegment equals 0 for a single-segment track.
The libraryService.ts shapeTrack() function's streamUrl output is not asserted in any test to verify it returns the correct URL format after the endpoint migration.
No end-to-end test exercises the full seek flow: playlist reload with startSegment > 0, followed by segment fetch at the correct index, followed by correct position reporting in the Flutter client.
The _segmentDuration constant in the Flutter client is never tested against the actual backend HLS_SEGMENT_SECONDS value to detect drift.
